title: API Performance Optimization Guide
content:
## API Performance Troubleshooting Guide

### Common Causes
1. Database queries not using indexes — check slow query logs
2. Connection pool exhausted — check if current connections exceed max_connections
3. Third-party dependency timeout — check downstream service response times

### Emergency Measures
- Increase API gateway timeout threshold to 30 seconds
- Enable query cache (Redis)
- Contact DBA to check database load

### Escalation Criteria
- If VIP customer and core business impacted, escalate directly to P1 and notify tech director
- If scope of impact exceeds 10 customers, initiate incident response process

title: Data Export Fault Troubleshooting
content:
## Data Export Fault Troubleshooting

### Problem Description
Timeout or 500 error when exporting large amounts of data.

### Troubleshooting Steps
1. Check export data volume — over 500K rows requires batch export
2. Check database temporary tablespace — large queries need temporary space
3. Check export queue backlog — too many concurrent export tasks

### Temporary Solutions
- Advise customer to narrow time range and export in batches
- Backend can manually trigger offline export task

### Escalation Criteria
- If a paid feature is erroring, must fix within 24 hours

title: User Permission Batch Management Manual
content:
## Batch Permission Management

### Method 1: Admin Console Batch Operation
1. Go to Admin Console -> User Management -> Batch Operations
2. Select user group or filter by criteria
3. Click 'Batch Modify Permissions'
4. Select target permission template

### Method 2: API Batch Operation
- Endpoint: POST /api/v2/users/batch-update
- Parameters: user_ids[], role_id
- Limit: maximum 500 users per request

### Notes
- Batch operations are irreversible, recommend testing in staging environment first
- Admin permissions cannot be batch modified

title: Data Sync Fault Troubleshooting and Recovery
content:
## Data Sync Fault Troubleshooting

### Common Causes
1. Message queue backlog — Kafka consumer lag too high
2. Network partition — cross-region sync affected by network
3. Target system unavailable — check downstream service health

### Emergency Measures
- Check Kafka consumer group status
- Restart data sync worker
- If not recovered after 1 hour, initiate manual data reconciliation

### Escalation Criteria
- VIP customer data sync delay over 30 minutes -> escalate to P1
- Financial data involved -> escalate to P0 and notify risk control team

title: Ticket Priority and SLA Standards
content:
## Ticket Priority Definitions

| Level | Response Time | Resolution Time | Applicable Scenarios |
|-------|--------------|----------------|---------------------|
| P0-Critical | 15 minutes | 2 hours | System completely unavailable |
| P1-High | 1 hour | 8 hours | Core functionality affected |
| P2-Medium | 4 hours | 24 hours | Non-core functionality issues |
| P3-Low | 24 hours | 72 hours | Usage inquiries / minor issues |

## VIP Customer Acceleration Rules
- VIP customer tickets are automatically escalated one priority level
- VIP customer P1 and above issues require sync-notification to customer success manager

## Renewal Period Customer Special Handling
- Customers within 60 days of contract expiration have ticket priority automatically escalated one level
- Must sync-notify sales team for attention
